title: B-1: registerProjectTools scaffold + schedule_item + delete_scheduled_item
value: Claude can create and delete ScheduledItems for a project goal (tasks, milestones, launch steps, reviews), enabling the first plannable unit of work on any non-fitness goal.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file `src/lib/mcp/tools/project-tools.ts` exports `registerProjectTools(server: McpServer): void`.
`registerAll` in `src/lib/mcp/tools.ts` imports and calls `registerProjectTools(server)` after `registerWriteTools`.
The existing `decodeArgsDeep` monkey-patch (applied at the top of `registerAll`) covers `schedule_item` and `delete_scheduled_item` automatically — no extra decode call needed in the new file.

`tools/list` response includes `schedule_item` with a typed Zod inputSchema: `goalId: string`, `date: DateKeyShape (yyyy-mm-dd)`, `type: string.min(1) (open enum: 'task'|'milestone'|'launch-step'|'review')`, `title: string.min(1).max(500)`, `detail?: string`, `payload?: unknown`, `externalRef?: string`.
`schedule_item` description explicitly states it is for non-fitness / project goals and NOT for logging fitness workouts, hikes, or baselines.

`tools/call schedule_item` with a valid project goalId, `date='2026-06-01'`, `type='milestone'`, `title='Submit to App Store'` creates a `ScheduledItem` row; verify via `prisma.scheduledItem.findFirst` in a Node REPL.
Date input uses `parseDateInput` (already defined in `tools.ts`) so bare `yyyy-mm-dd` is USER_TZ midnight, not UTC midnight.
Return shape: `{ id: string, goalId: string, date: string, type: string, title: string, status: 'planned', message: string }`.
`tools/list` includes `delete_scheduled_item` with `inputSchema: { id: string }`.
`delete_scheduled_item` description states it is for project goal ScheduledItems only.
`tools/call delete_scheduled_item` with a valid id hard-deletes the row; a second call returns a user-friendly error (not an unhandled Prisma throw).
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero new errors.
`npm run build` succeeds.
NOTE: claude.ai connector reload required after merging — the tool list changed.
dependsOn: Add ScheduledItem + LogEntry Prisma models (Epic A)

title: B-2: complete_item + update_scheduled_item
value: Claude can mark a ScheduledItem done (completing a task or milestone) and patch its metadata (reschedule, rename, change type/status), enabling lifecycle management of project work items.
acceptanceCriteria: `tools/list` includes `complete_item` with `inputSchema: { id: string, completedAt?: string (ISO datetime; defaults to now in USER_TZ) }`.
`complete_item` description states it sets `status='done'` and stamps `completedAt`; clarifies it is for project goal ScheduledItems, not fitness logs.
`tools/call complete_item` with a valid ScheduledItem id sets `status='done'` and `completedAt` on the row; verify via DB query.

When `completedAt` is omitted, `completedAt` defaults to current time (using `new Date()`, not a date-only parse); when supplied as `yyyy-mm-dd`, `parseDateInput` is used so it lands at USER_TZ midnight.
Return shape: `{ id: string, status: 'done', completedAt: string (ISO), message: string }`.
`tools/list` includes `update_scheduled_item` with PATCH-style inputSchema: `{ id: string, title?: string, detail?: string, date?: DateKeyShape, status?: string, type?: string }`.
`update_scheduled_item` description clarifies it is for project goal ScheduledItems and lists editable fields.
`tools/call update_scheduled_item` with `{ id, title: 'New title' }` updates only `title`; other fields are unchanged (true PATCH, not replace-all).
If `date` is provided, it goes through `parseDateInput` → `startOfDay` to enforce USER_TZ midnight.
Calling either tool with an id that does not exist returns a user-friendly error result (not a raw Prisma `RecordNotFound` throw).
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
`npm run build` succeeds.
NOTE: claude.ai connector reload required after merging.

title: B-3: list_scheduled_items
value: Claude can query a goal's scheduled work across any date range and filter by status, enabling 'what's planned this week', 'what milestones are still open', and milestone burn-down coaching prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: `tools/list` includes `list_scheduled_items` with inputSchema: `{ goalId: string, from?: DateKeyShape, to?: DateKeyShape, status?: z.enum(['planned','done','skipped']), type?: string, limit?: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50) }`.

`list_scheduled_items` description explicitly states it queries ScheduledItems for project / non-fitness goals, lists filter params, and gives example use cases ('what's planned this sprint', 'open milestones', 'milestone burn-down').
When `from`/`to` are provided they are parsed via `parseDateInput` → `startOfDay`/`endOfDay` for USER_TZ correctness.
When `goalId` is provided but the goal does not exist, a user-friendly error is returned (not a raw throw).
When no filters are provided, returns the most recent `limit` items ordered by `date desc`.
When `status='planned'` is passed, only rows with `status='planned'` are returned.
Return shape: `{ count: number, items: { id, goalId, date, type, title, detail, status, completedAt, externalRef, createdAt }[] }`.
Smoke: call `list_scheduled_items` for the chewgether test goal after seeding 3 items → response `count` matches DB row count.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
`npm run build` succeeds.
NOTE: claude.ai connector reload required after merging.

title: B-4: log_metric + list_log_entries
value: Claude can record and query LogEntry observations (MRR snapshots, download counts, milestone counts, qualitative notes) for a project goal, feeding the readiness/progress system with real data.
acceptanceCriteria: `tools/list` includes `log_metric` with inputSchema: `{ goalId: string, metric: z.string().min(1).describe("Short key, e.g. 'mrr', 'downloads', 'milestones_done'"), value?: z.number(), text?: z.string(), date?: z.string().optional().describe('ISO datetime; default = now'), source?: z.enum(['manual','github','claude']).default('manual') }`.

`log_metric` description explicitly states it is for project / non-fitness goals, gives examples ('log MRR: metric=mrr value=450', 'log milestone completion: metric=milestones_done value=3'), and states it is NOT for fitness measurements (use log_measurement for weight/body fat).
At least one of `value` or `text` must be provided; the tool returns a user-friendly error if both are omitted.
When `date` is omitted, `createdAt`/`date` defaults to `new Date()` (current instant, not midnight).
When `date` is provided as `yyyy-mm-dd`, `parseDateInput` is used for USER_TZ correctness.
Return shape: `{ id: string, goalId: string, metric: string, value: number|null, text: string|null, date: string, source: string, message: string }`.
`tools/call log_metric` with `{ goalId, metric: 'mrr', value: 450 }` creates a LogEntry row; verify row in DB.
`tools/list` includes `list_log_entries` with inputSchema: `{ goalId: string, metric?: string, from?: z.string(), to?: z.string(), limit?: z.number().int().min(1).max(500).default(50) }`.
`list_log_entries` description states it queries LogEntry observations for project goals, explains it can filter by metric key, and gives 'list_log_entries(metric=mrr)' as an example for MRR trend.
Return shape: `{ count: number, entries: { id, goalId, date, metric, value, text, source, createdAt }[] }` ordered by `date desc`.
Smoke: `list_log_entries` for the chewgether test goal after logging 2 MRR entries → `count: 2`, entries sorted newest-first.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
`npm run build` succeeds.
NOTE: claude.ai connector reload required after merging.
dependsOn: Add ScheduledItem + LogEntry Prisma models (Epic A)

title: B-5: get_today_plan project-goal branch (today's ScheduledItems in plan response)
value: When claude.ai calls get_today_plan and the active goal is a project goal, the response includes today's ScheduledItems so Claude can open coaching sessions with a grounded project view without an extra tool call.
acceptanceCriteria: `get_today_plan` return shape gains a `todayItems` field: `{ id, type, title, status, completedAt }[]` — present for project goals (non-empty when items exist today), empty array for fitness goals (zero regression).

The query is a parallel `prisma.scheduledItem.findMany({ where: { goalId: activeGoal.id, date: { gte: todayStart, lte: todayEnd } }, orderBy: { date: 'asc' } })` added to the existing `resolveDay` call or the `get_today_plan` handler — does NOT touch `resolveDay` logic, does NOT alter existing fitness fields.
For a fitness active goal (kind='fitness'), `todayItems` is `[]` and no `ScheduledItem` query is issued (guard on `goal.kind === 'project'`).
For a project active goal (kind='project'), `todayItems` reflects actual DB rows for today in USER_TZ.
`activeGoal` field (added in Epic A) must already be present in the response; this story adds `todayItems` alongside it.

Smoke: curl `tools/call get_today_plan` with the fitness goal active → response has `todayItems: []`; after activating a test project goal and scheduling an item for today → `todayItems` has that item.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
`npm run build` succeeds.
NOTE: No connector reload needed (get_today_plan is an existing tool; its return shape is additive — claude.ai does not cache output shapes).
touches: src/lib/mcp/tools.ts (get_today_plan handler or resolveDay call site)
dependsOn: Add ScheduledItem + LogEntry Prisma models and get_today_plan activeGoal field (Epic A)

title: B-6: Epic B integration smoke + Sprint 2 QA gate
value: Verifies the full project tool pack end-to-end before merging to main, confirming all 7 tools are discoverable, the fitness path is untouched, and the codebase is type-clean and buildable.
acceptanceCriteria: curl `tools/list` response includes exactly these 7 new tools: `schedule_item`, `complete_item`, `update_scheduled_item`, `delete_scheduled_item`, `list_scheduled_items`, `log_metric`, `list_log_entries`.
None of the 7 new tools appear in the fitness-focused description fields of existing tools (no name collision, no confusion).
Create a test chewgether goal via MCP (`create_goal` with `kind='project'`) — `list_goals` response includes it with `kind='project'`.
End-to-end flow: `schedule_item` (milestone) → `list_scheduled_items` (status=planned) → `complete_item` → `list_scheduled_items` (status=done) — all return correct data.
End-to-end flow: `log_metric` (metric='mrr', value=450) → `list_log_entries` (metric='mrr') → entry appears.
`get_today_plan` with the project goal active returns `todayItems` populated for today's scheduled item.
Switch active goal back to the fitness goal → `get_today_plan` returns fitness-identical output with `todayItems: []`; no fitness tool behavior changed.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors.
`npm run build` succeeds.
claude.ai connector reload performed; new tools visible in claude.ai's tool picker.
touches: No code changes — QA-only story. Documents the curl commands and expected outputs as runbook for the developer.
dependsOn: B-5: get_today_plan project-goal branch (today's ScheduledItems in plan response)
